2606.23588.title: A Generative Model for Closed-Loop Microsimulation of Signalized Intersections
2606.23588.paper_summary: Traffic microsimulators rely on hand-crafted behavior models that reproduce aggregate flow but miss the heterogeneous interactions between vehicles at signalized intersections. Learned trajectory predictors capture richer interactions but are short-horizon and tend to be unstable when run in closed loop. We present Enactor, an actor-centric generative model for closed-loop intersection microsimulation. The model focuses on vehicles; pedestrians are included as context that can influence vehicle decisions but not predicted. Dynamic actors and lane polylines are encoded in polar coordinates referenced to the intersection center. A transformer with separate spatial and temporal attention blocks predicts a distribution over each actor's next-step motion ($s$, $α$). Training uses a closed-loop curriculum so the model is exposed to its own predictions. We evaluate Enactor in two regimes. In a 4000-second simulation-in-the-loop test at two intersection geometries, Enactor controls every dynamic vehicle against a continuously refreshing actor set rather than the fixed cohort that learned trajectory predictors are usually evaluated against. It recovers the SUMO data generator's speed and travel-time distributions with KL divergence over an order of magnitude lower than a recent transformer baseline on travel time, and substantially lower on speed (roughly $5\times$ lower at Site 1), and reduces red-light violations relative to the same baseline by more than an order of magnitude. An ablation isolates the leader rear-bumper feature as the change with the largest effect on intersection-aware safety metrics. We also evaluate on real-world field data and apply the same architecture to naturalistic vehicle trajectories from a fish-eye camera at a signalized intersection and evaluate it on multi-horizon predictive tasks. Enactor outperforms a constant-velocity baseline at every horizon evaluated.
2606.23588.paper_summary_zh: 交通微觀模擬器依賴手工製作的行為模型，這些模型能再現總體流量，但忽略了信號交叉口處車輛之間的異質互動。學習的軌跡預測器捕捉到更豐富的互動，但其預測範圍較短，且在閉環運行時往往不穩定。我們提出了 Enactor，一種以行為者為中心的生成模型，用於閉環交叉口微觀模擬。該模型專注於車輛；行人作為影響車輛決策的上下文被納入考量，但不進行預測。動態行為者和車道多邊形以參考交叉口中心的極坐標編碼。具有獨立空間和時間注意力模塊的Transformer預測每個行為者下一步運動的分佈 ($s$, $α$)。訓練使用閉環課程，使模型能夠接觸到自己的預測。我們在兩種情境下評估 Enactor。在一個 4000 秒的模擬循環測試中，針對兩個交叉口幾何形狀，Enactor 控制每一個動態車輛，與不斷更新的行為者集進行對抗，而不是通常用於評估學習軌跡預測器的固定隊列。它恢復了 SUMO 數據生成器的速度和旅行時間分佈，KL 散度比最近的Transformer基準低一個量級，旅行時間的表現顯著低於基準（在 Site 1 大約低 $5\times$），並且相對於相同基準減少了紅燈違規的次數，超過一個量級的改善。一個消融實驗將領導者後保險杠特徵孤立出來，作為對交叉口安全指標影響最大的變化。我們還在真實世界的現場數據上進行評估，並將相同的架構應用於信號交叉口的魚眼相機自然車輛軌跡，並在多時間範圍預測任務上進行評估。Enactor 在每個評估的時間範圍內均優於恆速基準。
2606.23588.author: Yash Ranjan et.al.
2606.23588.authors: Yash Ranjan, Rahul Sengupta, Anand Rangarajan, Sanjay Ranka
